citation.ref: LNG FY2025 10-K open-position sensitivity + S3 recontracting/supply-wave

consensus_variance.line_item: FY2027 Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA durability + multiple
consensus_variance.narrative: The Street's +27% median PT (~$304.50) extrapolates the raised FY2026 run-rate (a spread/optimization-driven raise) into a sustained ~15-17x forward multiple and capitalizes the full pre-FID growth pipeline. Our downward variance marks the raise as partly transient (merchant lever <1 mtpa, <$50M per $1/MMBtu), the marginal book recontracting/FID-ing into the supply wave at plausibly lower fixed fees (~$2/MMBtu vs legacy ~$3/MMBtu), and base-rate multiple compression for premium energy-infra into oversupply; it shifts ~12pp from base/bull toward the bear tails. Two-tailed (AI-power gas demand + premium SPAs/FIDs are genuine upside) -> Hold not Sell.

revision_velocity.verdict: De-rate risk (full multiple into oversupply), NOT estimate deterioration.
